notable_pick_trades: Traded 2026 2nd and 4th round picks to Houston Texans for LT Laremy Tunsil (also sent 2025 3rd and 7th). May receive a compensatory 6th-round pick.
roster_context: Disappointing 5-12 season after NFC Championship appearance in 2024. Jayden Daniels missed 10 games with injuries. Defense was bottom-three in the NFL. New DC Daronte Jones (from Minnesota Vikings) installing aggressive, multiple-front scheme. New OC David Blough. 29 unrestricted free agents. Over $90M in projected cap space after Lattimore and Biadasz releases. Team needs a near-complete defensive overhaul while adding weapons around Daniels on offense.

needs.explanation: Tyler Biadasz was shockingly released in February, creating a hole at center. Nick Allegretti (re-signed 1-year extension) is the default center but is below-average and struggled with pass protection in 2025. Chris Paul (LG) is an impending UFA with a bigger market than expected. Brandon Coleman is the likely LG replacement if Paul leaves. The tackles are set (Tunsil, Conerly) and Sam Cosmi is locked in at RG, but center is now a real problem. This is a weak center draft class, so free agency is the likely avenue.
needs.current_starter: LG: Chris Paul (UFA) / Brandon Coleman; C: Nick Allegretti (by default); RG: Sam Cosmi
needs.depth: Andrew Wylie (re-signed, versatile backup), Brandon Coleman can play guard. Center depth is nonexistent.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 5-6 for depth; center likely addressed in free agency

needs.explanation: Terry McLaurin is the only established weapon. Deebo Samuel is a UFA after a lackluster one-year stint (72 catches, 727 yards, 5 TDs). Only 3-4 WRs are under contract. Luke McCaffrey and Jaylin Lane are unproven young pieces. Treylon Burks is a depth option. Washington needs a true WR2 to pair with McLaurin to support Jayden Daniels. Brandon Aiyuk (expected SF cut) is a free agent target, but draft investment likely still needed at slot WR.
needs.depth: Jaylin Lane (slot), Treylon Burks — no established WR2 behind McLaurin
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-5 if addressed via free agency (Aiyuk), Round 1-2 if not

needs.explanation: Marshon Lattimore was released, clearing $18.5M in cap space. He was ranked 96th out of 114 qualifying CBs by PFF in 2025. Jonathan Jones and Noah Igbinoghene are UFAs. Trey Amos (broken leg Week 10) and Mike Sainristil (struggled badly in Year 2) are under contract but neither is a proven CB1. The Commanders allowed the third-worst yards per pass attempt in the NFL (8.1). Washington is expected to be aggressive pursuing a CB in free agency but still needs draft capital here.
needs.current_starter: Mike Sainristil, Trey Amos (returning from broken leg)
needs.depth: Very thin — Sainristil regressed significantly, Amos is unproven beyond 10 games. No other viable options on roster.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3 (pick 71) if addressed heavily in free agency, Round 1 if not

needs.explanation: Daron Payne has a $28M cap hit (largest on the roster) and may be restructured or cut. Javon Kinlaw is under contract on a 3-year deal. Johnny Newton showed flashes but is still developing. Shy Tuttle was re-signed on a 1-year extension. Eddie Goldman and Sheldon Day are UFAs. The IDL group is adequate but could lose Payne, and Jones' 3-4 scheme requires stout run-stuffing bodies up front. Mid-level free agents and late-round depth are likely the approach.
needs.current_starter: Daron Payne (potential cut/restructure), Javon Kinlaw, Jer'Zhan Newton
needs.depth: Shy Tuttle (re-signed), Ricky Barber. Goldman and Day are UFAs.

needs.explanation: Bobby Wagner (36, UFA) played the most defensive snaps on the team in 2025 and is likely gone. Frankie Luvu is under contract and fits Jones' scheme as a hybrid blitzer/LB, but regressed in 2025. Jordan Magee, Kain Medrano, and Ale Kaho are young but unproven depth pieces. Jones' scheme from Minnesota requires athletic, versatile LBs who can blitz and cover — Washington lacks that profile outside of Luvu. Need a true MIKE/WILL to replace Wagner's role.
needs.current_starter: Frankie Luvu (under contract), Bobby Wagner (UFA — likely gone)
needs.depth: Jordan Magee, Kain Medrano, Ale Kaho — young developmental pieces
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (Arvell Reese, Sonny Styles if not going EDGE)

needs.explanation: Austin Ekeler, Jeremy McNichols, and Chase Edmonds are all UFAs. Jacory Croskey-Merritt (7th-round pick) is the only RB under contract for certain. Chris Rodriguez Jr. is an RFA likely to return. The coaching staff has expressed confidence in Croskey-Merritt but this is a very thin room. Dan Quinn was asked about taking RB Jeremiyah Love at No. 7 and did not rule it out. A veteran signing plus mid-round pick is the likely approach.
needs.depth: Essentially no depth behind those two. Ekeler, McNichols, Edmonds all UFAs.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (if Love falls and defense addressed in FA) or Round 3-5

needs.explanation: The single biggest need on the roster. Von Miller (37, UFA) led the team with 10 sacks in 2025 but is a free agent. Dorance Armstrong is the only edge rusher of note under contract but missed significant time with a Week 7 injury. Deatrich Wise was re-signed but is a run-setting DE, not a true pass rusher. Jalyn Holmes, Drake Jackson, Jacob Martin, and Preston Smith are all free agents. The Commanders have no young, dynamic edge presence. GM Adam Peters specifically highlighted pass rusher as an area of need. Daniel Jeremiah called it the biggest need for this team.
needs.current_starter: Dorance Armstrong (under contract), Deatrich Wise (re-signed 1-year extension)
needs.depth: Javontae Jean-Baptiste, T.J. Maguranyanga — extremely thin. Frankie Luvu could rush from LB but is not a true edge.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 — top target at No. 7 overall (David Bailey, Rueben Bain Jr., or Arvell Reese)

needs.explanation: Widely considered a top-3 need. Will Harris suffered a fractured fibula in Week 3 and was ineffective upon return. Quan Martin regressed significantly and was one of the worst-graded safeties in the NFL. Percy Butler and Tyler Owens are special teamers. Jeremy Reaves is a special teams ace, not a defensive starter. Daronte Jones plans to run three-safety looks (like Minnesota), meaning Washington needs multiple upgrades. The team had only 8 interceptions in 2025, fourth-worst in the NFL.
needs.current_starter: Will Harris, Quan Martin (both on final year of deals)
needs.depth: Percy Butler, Tyler Owens, Jeremy Reaves — all better suited for special teams. No starter-caliber depth.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (Caleb Downs) if EDGE addressed in FA, or Round 3-5 if addressed via free agency

needs.explanation: Zach Ertz (torn ACL in December, age 35) is almost certainly done. John Bates is a strong blocking TE under contract. Ben Sinnott (2024 2nd-round pick) is expected to step into a larger role. Washington may target a pass-catching TE in free agency (Isaiah Likely, Dallas Goedert mentioned as targets). Not a top draft priority but could use another weapon.
needs.depth: Colson Yankoff. Ertz likely done after ACL at 35. Tyree Jackson is an RFA.

scheme_fit_notes: New DC Daronte Jones is installing a multiple, pressure-heavy scheme influenced by Brian Flores' Minnesota system. Expects to run hybrid 3-4/4-3 fronts with heavy blitzing on early downs. Three-safety looks will be featured prominently. Versatility is the key trait — Jones wants defenders who can play multiple roles (on-ball/off-ball LB, edge/OLB, safety/nickel). Pass rush will come from scheme pressure and blitz packages, but still needs at least one elite individual pass rusher off the edge. EDGE prospects with coverage versatility (like Arvell Reese) fit perfectly. Stout run-stuffing IDL are critical to enable two-deep safety coverage. Cornerbacks must be physical and comfortable in zone/match coverage concepts.
